h2h_summary: In the 6 recorded head-to-head matches between Portugal and Croatia, Portugal has won 4 times, Croatia has won 1 time, and they have drawn 1 time.
team_style_home: Portugal under Roberto Martinez favour a possession-based 4-3-3 that transitions into a 3-4-2-1, using technical midfield triangles and wide attackers to create chances while Ronaldo operates as the primary penalty-box finisher.
team_style_away: Croatia play a possession-based, midfield-controlled style under Zlatko Dalic, typically in a 4-3-3 or 3-4-2-1, relying on technical quality through the middle, a double pivot for defensive cover, and quick transitions from defense to attack.
wc_history_home: Portugal have appeared at nine World Cups, winning third place in 1966 led by Eusebio (Golden Boot), finishing fourth in 2006, and reaching the quarter-finals in 2022; they have never won the tournament.
wc_history_away: Croatia have been one of the most consistent World Cup performers since their debut, finishing third in 1998 and reaching the final in 2018 where they lost 4-2 to France, with Luka Modric winning the Golden Ball.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Elite technical midfield depth with Bruno Fernandes, Bernardo Silva, Vitinha, and João Neves all world-class
Tactical flexibility under Martinez — can shift between 4-3-3 and 3-4-2-1 to adapt in-game
Proven winning mentality reinforced by 2025 UEFA Nations League title and Cristiano Ronaldo's leadership
strengths_away: Elite midfield depth anchored by Modric and Kovacic
Proven big-tournament mentality and knockout experience
Disciplined defensive organization and tactical flexibility
